title: ranunculus flammula var. ovalis
intro: Ranunculus flammula var. ovalis, also known as creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant with small, white or yellowish flowers that grow in wet environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus flammula var. ovalis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or topical cream to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
